div.head: 195. Telegram From the Department of State to the Embassy in the Republic of ChinaSource: Department of State, Central Files, 793.00/10–2058. Secret; Niact; Limit Distribution; No Distribution Outside Department. Drafted by Green, cleared by Parsons and Martin, and approved by Herter.
div.p: 369. Your 633.See footnote 1, Document 194. On October 6 Department questioned CNO’s office on advisability and necessity using LSD during cease-fire period.The conversation was recorded in a memorandum of October 7 from Green to Parsons. (Department of State, FE Files: Lot 63 D 76, Memoranda) Department informed LSD essential in delivering small landing craft needed in resupply especially of Tan islands. Department was assured that LSD would proceed without US escort to point about 15 miles offshore whence landing craft would swim ashore. LSD also to be used for landing craft pickup.

In view subsequent US convoy escort suspension announcement October 8,Cited in footnote 2, Document 165. would appreciate full explanation relating your comment on item one reftel.Drumright replied in telegram 642 from Taipei, October 21. (Department of State, Central Files, 790.5411/10–2158; see Supplement)
